questions.q: In which year was the film Raja Harishchandra released?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra was released in 1913, premiering publicly in Bombay that May.

questions.q: Who directed the 1913 film Raja Harishchandra?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra was written, produced and directed by Dhundiraj Govind Phalke, known as Dadasaheb Phalke.

questions.q: Raja Harishchandra (1913) is widely regarded as which milestone in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra is regarded as India's first full-length feature film.

questions.q: What type of film was the 1913 production Raja Harishchandra?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra was a silent film, made nearly two decades before India's first sound feature.

questions.q: The story of Raja Harishchandra (1913) belongs to which genre of Indian film?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra was a mythological film drawn from Hindu legend.

questions.q: Raja Harishchandra (1913) is based on the legend of a king famous for which virtue?
questions.explanation: The film is based on the legend of King Harishchandra, celebrated for his unwavering truthfulness.

questions.q: In Raja Harishchandra (1913), who played the female roles in the film?
questions.explanation: All roles, including the female ones, were played by men, as women were reluctant to act at the time.

questions.q: Who produced and financed the 1913 film Raja Harishchandra?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Phalke produced and financed Raja Harishchandra himself, writing, directing and producing the film.

questions.q: In which Indian city was Raja Harishchandra (1913) first publicly screened?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra had its first public screening in Bombay (present-day Mumbai).

questions.q: Dadasaheb Phalke, the maker of Raja Harishchandra (1913), is popularly honoured with which title?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Phalke is widely called the Father of Indian Cinema for pioneering films like Raja Harishchandra.

questions.q: Because Raja Harishchandra (1913) was a silent film, how was its narrative conveyed to audiences?
options: Through a radio broadcast played alongside
questions.explanation: As a silent film, Raja Harishchandra relied on intertitle cards and live musical accompaniment rather than recorded sound.

questions.q: In the legend dramatized in Raja Harishchandra (1913), which sage tests King Harishchandra by demanding his kingdom and wealth?
questions.explanation: In the legend, the sage Vishwamitra demands Harishchandra's kingdom and riches, setting off the king's trials.

questions.q: What is the name of King Harishchandra's wife, the queen depicted in the 1913 film Raja Harishchandra?
questions.explanation: Harishchandra's queen is named Taramati, who shares in the king's trials in the legend.

questions.q: Before turning to filmmaking and making Raja Harishchandra (1913), Dadasaheb Phalke worked primarily in which trade?
questions.explanation: Phalke had a background in printing, lithography and photography before he took up filmmaking.

questions.q: Which foreign film is often cited as the inspiration that prompted Dadasaheb Phalke to make Raja Harishchandra (1913)?
questions.explanation: Phalke was famously inspired after watching the film The Life of Christ, which led him to depict Indian gods and legends on screen.

questions.q: Raja Harishchandra (1913) was made roughly how many years before India's first sound feature film?
questions.explanation: India's first sound film, Alam Ara, came in 1931, about eighteen years after Raja Harishchandra.

questions.q: In which decade of cinema history did Dadasaheb Phalke produce Raja Harishchandra?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra was produced in 1913, placing it in the 1910s, the second decade of cinema.

questions.q: Which language tradition best describes the cultural source of Raja Harishchandra's (1913) story?
questions.explanation: The film drew on Hindu mythological and Puranic sources recounting the tale of the truthful King Harishchandra.

questions.q: India's highest award in cinema, named after the maker of Raja Harishchandra, is called what?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award, India's highest cinema honour, is named after Phalke, who directed Raja Harishchandra.

questions.q: In which year was the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, honouring the director of Raja Harishchandra, instituted?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award was instituted in 1969 to honour lifetime contribution to Indian cinema.

questions.q: A central trial of King Harishchandra in the 1913 film sees him forced to take up what lowly occupation?
options: A keeper of a cremation ground
questions.explanation: Stripped of his kingdom, Harishchandra is reduced to working at a cremation ground as part of his ordeal.

questions.q: On approximately which date did Raja Harishchandra (1913) have its first public commercial screening?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra is generally recorded as having its first public commercial screening on 3 May 1913 in Bombay.

questions.q: At which Bombay venue is the first public screening of Raja Harishchandra (1913) commonly said to have taken place?
questions.explanation: The first public screening is associated with the Coronation Cinematograph in Bombay.

questions.q: Which actor is generally credited with playing the title role of King Harishchandra in the 1913 film?
questions.explanation: Dattatraya Damodar Dabke is generally credited with portraying King Harishchandra in the film, while Anna Salunke played the queen Taramati.

questions.q: Anna Salunke, who acted in Raja Harishchandra (1913), is notable for later playing what unusual screen feat in Phalke's Lanka Dahan?
questions.explanation: Anna Salunke famously played both Rama and Sita in Phalke's Lanka Dahan, an early instance of an actor in a dual male and female role.

questions.q: Dadasaheb Phalke, director of Raja Harishchandra, was born in which year?
questions.explanation: Dhundiraj Govind Phalke, the director of Raja Harishchandra, was born in 1870.

questions.q: Over the course of his career, the maker of Raja Harishchandra, Dadasaheb Phalke, made roughly how many films?
questions.explanation: Phalke made around 95 films over roughly two decades following Raja Harishchandra.

questions.q: What was the approximate running length character of Raja Harishchandra (1913) that earned it the 'feature film' distinction?
options: A few minutes, like a newsreel
Roughly an hour of screen time across multiple reels
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra ran roughly an hour across several reels, making it a full-length feature rather than a short.

questions.q: Which challenge did Dadasaheb Phalke face in casting Raja Harishchandra (1913) that shaped its all-male cast?
options: Women's reluctance to appear on screen at the time
An objection from the film's financiers
questions.explanation: Women were reluctant to act in films at the time, so Phalke cast men in the female roles.

questions.q: Raja Harishchandra (1913) is most closely linked with the early film industry of which Indian city?
questions.explanation: Phalke produced and premiered Raja Harishchandra in Bombay, the cradle of the early Indian film industry.
